seo.title: Building Research Agents with Llama Stack + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build research agents with Llama Stack + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Llama Stack provides a standardized Python interface for building sophisticated Llama-powered research agents that handle web search, synthesis, and summarization workflows. Integrating Vex guardrails ensures reliability by verifying agent outputs against source accuracy, recency, and bias risks inherent in multi-step research pipelines. This guide shows how to wrap Llama Stack agent turns with vex.verify() to mitigate common failure modes in production research agents.

architectureOverview.heading: Research Agents Architecture with Llama Stack
architectureOverview.description:
Research agents built with Llama Stack follow a modular architecture featuring a central LlamaModel for inference, SafetyShield for content filtering, MemoryBank for conversation history and document retrieval, and ToolRegistry for web search and analysis tools. The agent operates in a loop: receiving user queries, retrieving relevant memory/documents, calling tools like web search APIs, processing responses through the Llama model, and applying safety checks before returning synthesized outputs.

Typical workflow involves query parsing → memory retrieval → tool execution (e.g., SerpAPI search) → multi-turn reasoning → final summary generation. MemoryBank maintains source documents and conversation context, while SafetyShield filters potentially harmful content. Vex integrates as post-processing to verify claims against retrieved sources.

This architecture excels at complex research tasks but introduces risks like hallucinated source associations and schema mismatches in tool responses.

risks.name: Safety shield filtering producing false positives on benign content
risks.description: Research queries about medical or controversial topics trigger false positives, blocking legitimate analysis
risks.mitigation: Wrap SafetyShield calls with Vex context-aware verification to override false positives on verified sources

risks.name: Agentic loop turn limits exceeded without meaningful resolution
risks.description: Multi-step research exceeds turn limits before converging on actionable insights
risks.mitigation: Use Vex to monitor reasoning progress and early-terminate unproductive loops

risks.name: Tool runtime responses not conforming to expected schema
risks.description: Web search APIs return malformed JSON, breaking agent parsing and synthesis
risks.mitigation: Validate tool responses with Vex schema enforcement before feeding to LlamaModel

risks.name: Memory bank retrieval hallucinating document associations
risks.description: Agent incorrectly associates claims with irrelevant or outdated retrieved documents
risks.mitigation: Vex source verification cross-checks claims against actual MemoryBank document content

implementation.title: Basic Llama Stack Research Agent Setup
implementation.description: Initialize Llama Stack client with memory and tools for research workflow
implementation.code:
from llama_stack_client import LlamaStack
from llama_stack_client.types import GenerateConfig

# Initialize stack with research capabilities
stack = LlamaStack(
    model='llama-3.2-11b-vision-instruct',
    memory_bank=True,
    safety_shield=True,
    tools=['serpapi_search', 'document_analyzer']
)

config = GenerateConfig(
    temperature=0.1,
    max_tokens=2048,
    tool_choice='auto'
)

response = stack.generate(
    'Research latest developments in quantum computing hardware',
    config=config
)
print(response.content)

vexIntegration.title: Source Verification Post-Processing
vexIntegration.description: Verify agent claims against MemoryBank retrieved documents
vexIntegration.code:
from vex_sdk import verify

# After Llama Stack agent turn
agent_output = stack.chat(conversation)

# Extract claims and sources from response
claims = extract_claims(agent_output.content)
sources = stack.memory_bank.retrieve(agent_output.content, limit=10)

verification = verify(
    claims=claims,
    sources=[doc['content'] for doc in sources],
    check_types=['source_verification', 'claim_accuracy'],
    context={'task': 'research_synthesis'}
)

if verification.passed:
    final_output = agent_output.content
else:
    final_output = f"VERIFIED SUMMARY: {verification.summary}"

vexIntegration.title: Recency and Bias Detection Wrapper
vexIntegration.description: Check source dates and detect biased language patterns

vexIntegration.title: Loop Termination with Vex Progress Monitoring
vexIntegration.description: Prevent infinite research loops using Vex reasoning quality scores
vexIntegration.code:
def monitored_research_loop(stack, query, max_turns=10):
    conversation = [{'role': 'user', 'content': query}]
    progress_scores = []
    
    for turn in range(max_turns):
        response = stack.chat(conversation)
        
        # Vex monitors reasoning progress
        progress = verify(
            text=response.content,
            previous_turns=conversation[-3:],  # Last 3 turns
            check_types=['reasoning_progress'],
            score_threshold=0.7
        )
        
        progress_scores.append(progress.score)
        
        if progress.passed and 'complete' in progress.categories:
            return response.content
        elif turn > 3 and sum(progress_scores[-3:]) / 3 < 0.6:
            return 'Research stalled - insufficient progress'
        
        conversation.extend([{'role': 'assistant', 'content': response.content}])
    
    return 'Turn limit exceeded'
